purpose: Predicts the temporal sequence and causal chain of financial stress episodes, identifying early warning signals and the inevitable policy response pattern
components: Phase 1 — Plumbing stress: funding market dysfunction (repo rates, FX swap basis) surfaces first because it is the foundational financing layer
Phase 2 — Leveraged unwind: positions dependent on cheap funding (basis trade, carry trades) lose financing and delever pro-cyclically via Brunnermeier-Pedersen liquidity spiral
Phase 3 — Central bank intervention: systemic necessity forces emergency facilities, asset purchases, or rate policy reversal within days to weeks
Phase 4 — Balance sheet ratchet: intervention mechanically expands balance sheets, which fail to fully normalize (0/6 historical episodes achieved full normalization)
Observable early warnings: FRA-OIS widening, repo rate volatility increase, reserve distribution concentration across banks
current_assessment: Pattern confirmed across four episodes (September 2019, March 2020, BoE 2022, ECB 2011-12) with day-level documentation. Currently in pre-Phase 1 with the basis trade at 3-4x March 2020 scale. The 0/4 disruption-free QT completion rate is offset by 45 months of successful QT under current conditions. The key uncertainty is whether NBFI intermediation alters the sequencing relative to bank-intermediated historical episodes, and whether Fed preemption modifies severity.
limitations: N=4 episodes, all in post-GFC era with active central bank backstops
NBFI migration may change propagation pathways and speed relative to bank-intermediated history
Fed preemption (demonstrated willingness to pause QT in 2019 and slow in 2024) could modify severity distribution
Framework describes sequence but not timing — lead times range from days to months across episodes
Does not incorporate dampening mechanisms (market adaptation, regulatory responses, central bank put)
